name: account.ldap
title: Account:LDAP
description: The declaration to configure a Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) account.

payload.content: An LDAP configuration defines an LDAP directory account for a user.

payloadkeys.title: Account Name
payloadkeys.content: The name that apps show to the user for this LDAP account. If not present, the system generates a suitable default.

payloadkeys.title: Server Host Name
payloadkeys.content: The hostname or IP address of the LDAP server.

payloadkeys.title: Server Port
payloadkeys.content: The port number or IP address of the LDAP server.

payloadkeys.title: Authentication Credentials Asset Reference
payloadkeys.content: The identifier of an asset declaration that contains the credentials for this account. Set the corresponding asset type to `CredentialUserNameAndPassword`.

payloadkeys.title: Search Settings
payloadkeys.content: The array of nodes to start LDAP searches from. There must be at least one node for this account to be useful. macOS only searches one node and ignores other items in the array.

subkeys.title: Visible Description
subkeys.content: The description of this search setting in the Contacts and Settings apps. If not present, the apps display no name.

subkeys.title: Search Base
subkeys.content: The path to the node where a search starts. For example, `ou=people,o=example corp`.

subkeys.title: Scope
subkeys.content:
The type of recursion to use in the search:

- `Base`: The search uses only the `SearchBase` node.
- `OneLevel`: The search uses the `SearchBase` node and its immediate children.
- `Subtree`: The search uses the `SearchBase` node and all its children, regardless of depth.
